Case Study 01

Centralized
Event Hub

Transforming event registration from a fragmented transaction into a scalable product ecosystem — unifying discovery, state management, and cross-brand engagement.

Product Strategy UX Design Enterprise Platform Design
3
Brands Unified
3-Phase
Rollout Strategy
↓
Returning-User Friction
Scroll
My Role
Lead Product Designer
Domain
Federal, Defense & SLED Events
Scope
Multi-brand Platform Redesign
Outcome
Scalable Retention Platform
Background

From fragmented
to unified

The organization operates high-volume industry events across multiple brands serving Federal, Defense, and SLED audiences. Over time, each brand evolved its own registration flow through incremental page-level optimizations — with no unified system tying them together.


This created a compounding set of structural problems that no amount of single-page optimization could fix.

Behavioral data revealed
the real story

3.2×
Repeat Attendance Rate
Average user attended more than 3 events per year across brands — but the system had no cross-brand awareness of this.
4.7×
Pre-conversion Page Visits
Users visited event pages nearly 5 times on average before registering — each visit requiring re-identification from scratch.
100%
Redundant Data Entry
Every registration across every brand required full contact re-entry — no recognition, no profile, no memory of the user.
Strategy

From page optimization
to platform thinking

Engineering projected a one-year build for the full Event Hub. Rather than waiting or abandoning the vision, I proposed a phased rollout strategy that delivered value immediately while building toward the platform.

01
Phase One
Reduce Friction

Fixed waitlist clarity, redundant data entry, and ambiguous confirmation states.

02
Phase Two
Behavioral Onboarding

Introduced Hub-aligned patterns without disrupting existing flows.

03
Phase Three
Activate the Hub

Post-registration confirmation became the entry point — auto-recognized, no re-entry, magic link access.

Key Insight

The Hub activated at peak intent — post-registration — turning a transaction into a platform entry point.

Design

Designing the
ecosystem

Key screens and flows from the Centralized Event Hub — from the unified discovery layer to the confirmation-page activation moment.

Unified Event Dashboard
A single view aggregating upcoming registrations, waitlist status, and recommended events across all brands — with no duplicate login or data entry required.
Streamlined Registration
Existing flow preserved — with auto-fill for returning users, clear waitlist messaging, and a frictionless confirmation experience.
Magic Link Activation
Post-confirmation, users are introduced to their Hub via magic link — no password, no separate sign-up. Status auto-recognized from ticket data.
Outcome

From transaction
to relationship

↓
Returning-User Friction
Eliminated redundant data entry and cross-brand re-identification for all returning users.
3×
Brands Unified
Shared registration infrastructure across Federal, Defense, and SLED — no disruption to existing flows.
0→1
Platform Layer
Built the foundational layer for repeat engagement, cross-brand promotion, and long-term retention.

Shifting from page optimization to ecosystem design delivered compounding returns — reduced friction, cross-brand infrastructure, and events repositioned as a repeat product experience.

"This reframed the conversation with Product and Engineering — from incremental UI improvements to platform strategy."

— Rania Tirari, Lead Product Designer
